title: Conditional HTTP Fetching, Redirects, Compression, and Publisher Budgets
description: Every poll downloads a full feed, validators are overwritten on failure, redirects bypass scope, compressed bytes expand without limit, and retries synchronize across publishers. Learners cumulatively produce publisher-friendly conditional fetch client through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
Classify 200, 304, redirect, temporary, permanent, auth, missing, rate-limit, and unexpected responses before XML admission.
Reapply scheme, authority, address, hop, credential, and loop policy at each redirect and record permanent moves separately.
